scenario_label: Earthquake aftermath, simulated Cianjur West Java context, 2-hour window
context_note: Strong shallow earthquake. 15 reports across ~3km radius. Severity range 2-5 with one catastrophic building collapse, multiple priority zones, compound hazards (structural + gas + fire risk). Designed to exercise hardest-case synthesis with explicit conflict resolution and triage prioritization.
status: SKELETON — to be filled out Day 2 with 15 full reports following the pattern of scenario_a. Three seed reports below establish the time window and geographic anchors.

reports.severity_rationale: Two-story concrete building pancake-collapsed; arm visible protruding from rubble; active dust cloud; small fire at one corner indicating possible gas leak.
reports.immediate_action: Mark location for heavy rescue team; do not enter rubble; deploy fire suppression if extinguisher available without entering structure; isolate gas supply at street level.
reports.routing_rationale: Severity 5 with confirmed trapped person and compound hazards.

reports.severity_rationale: Three single-story houses partially collapsed; visible cracks running through remaining walls; one elderly resident standing in shock outside, holding head.
reports.immediate_action: Move residents from beside damaged structures to open area; check elderly woman for head injury; clear road for emergency access.
reports.routing_rationale: Severity 4, vulnerable persons in continuing-collapse risk area.

reports.severity_rationale: School courtyard with cracked perimeter wall; ~30 children evacuated outside; teachers conducting headcount; one child with bandaged scrape.
reports.immediate_action: Confirm headcount; move children further from cracked wall; prepare bus or large vehicle for transport to staging area; alert parents.
reports.routing_rationale: Large vulnerable group (children) in damaged structure proximity.
